topic: OpenAI Hosted Shell and Apply Patch: GPT-5.5 Compute Tools for Autonomous Code Execution
keyword: openai hosted shell apply patch gpt-5.5

key_points: Hosted shell gives models a complete terminal environment with controlled internet access
Agents can install packages via pip, npm, or apt, run multi-step shell scripts, and write file artifacts
Artifacts produced under /mnt/data are retrievable via the container/files API
Available through Responses API only (not Chat Completions API)
container_auto option lets OpenAI provision and manage containers automatically
apply_patch tool lets GPT-5.1+ create, update, and delete files using structured unified diffs
Response includes apply_patch_call objects describing each file operation
Works in conjunction with shell tool for agentic file discovery and editing
Supports multi-file refactors, bug fixes, test/doc generation, and mechanical migrations
Built-in capability in GPT-5.1 and above — no manual tool registration needed
Shell + Skills + Compaction pattern extends long-running agent reliability
Skills enable agents to reuse pre-built capabilities across tasks
Compaction reduces token usage while preserving agent context
Recommended approach for agents doing real, multi-step work
Covers best practices for chaining shell commands and structured outputs
GPT-5.5 is the recommended model for most Codex tasks in 2026
Especially strong for implementation, refactors, debugging, testing, and knowledge-work artifacts
Integrates natively with OpenAI Agents SDK for orchestrating multi-step coding workflows
Token-efficient: matches GPT-5.4 latency while using fewer tokens on the same Codex tasks
Supports background parallel task execution in the Codex cloud environment
Codex prompting guide covers best practices for structured diff-based editing
Recommends combining shell exploration with apply_patch for accurate multi-file edits
Using file-system greps and reads before patching reduces hallucination in diffs
Covers sandbox setup, networking controls, and artifact retrieval patterns
Provides prompt templates for iterative, self-correcting coding loops

stats.fact: GPT-5.5 achieves 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, a state-of-the-art score for complex command-line planning and tool coordination
GPT-5.5 resolves 58.6% of real-world GitHub issues on SWE-Bench Pro in a single attempt
GPT-5.5 ranks #2 out of 112 models in agentic tool use and computer tasks benchmarks with an average score of 99.2
GPT-5.5 reaches 78.7% on OSWorld-Verified (computer use) vs. 78.0% for the nearest competitor
In code review benchmarks, GPT-5.5 agents found 79.2% of expected issues vs. 58.3% baseline, with precision improving from 27.9% to 40.6%
GPT-5.5 scores 81.8% on CyberGym (security-focused agentic task benchmark) vs. 73.1% for Claude Opus 4.7
GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro became available in the OpenAI API on April 24, 2026

angles: Step-by-step setup guide: configuring hosted shell + apply_patch in the Responses API with real code examples
Comparing hosted shell vs. local shell: when to use each and security trade-offs
Building an end-to-end autonomous PR agent using GPT-5.5, hosted shell, and apply_patch
Token efficiency gains of GPT-5.5 over GPT-5.4 for Codex-style coding tasks
How hosted shell artifacts (files under /mnt/data) integrate with downstream pipelines
Security best practices: domain allowlisting, sandboxing, and risk mitigation for hosted shell agents
keywords: openai shell tool vs local shell
